name: jenkins
label: Jenkins
description: Jenkins is an open-source automation server widely used to implement Continuous Integration (CI) and Continuous Delivery (CD) pipelines. It helps automate the parts of software development related to building, testing, and deploying, facilitating faster and more reliable software delivery.

fields.title: Server URL
fields.name: server_url
fields.description: Provide the host URL of the jenkins server to connect and perform automated operations.
fields.tooltip: Provide the host URL of the jenkins jenkins to connect and perform automated operations.

fields.title: Username
fields.name: username
fields.description: Username of the jenkins server to which you will connect and perform automated operations.
fields.tooltip: Username of the jenkins server to which you will connect and perform automated operations.

fields.title: API Token
fields.name: api_token
fields.description: Specify the API token of the jenkins server to which you will connect and perform automated operations.
fields.tooltip: Specify the API token of the jenkins server to which you will connect and perform automated operations.

fields.title: Verify SSL
fields.name: verify_ssl
fields.description: Specifies whether the SSL certificate for the server is to be verified. By default, this option is selected, i.e., set to true.
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the SSL certificate for for the server is to be verified or not. By default, this option is set as True.

operations.title: Trigger Job
operations.description: Triggers a Jenkins job with specified parameters.

parameters.title: Job Name
parameters.name: job_name
parameters.description: Specify the name of the Jenkins job to be triggered.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the name of the Jenkins job to be triggered.

parameters.name: build_parameters
parameters.title: Build Parameters
parameters.description: Specify the parameters in JSON format as key-value pairs to be passed to the job
parameters.tooltip: Specify the parameters in JSON format as key-value pairs to be passed to the job

operations.title: Get Job Status
operations.description: Retrieves the current status and details of the specified Jenkins job.

parameters.title: Job Name
parameters.name: job_name
parameters.description: Specify the name of the Jenkins job to retrieve its status and detailed information.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the name of the Jenkins job to retrieve its status and detailed information.

parameters.title: Build Number
parameters.name: build_number
parameters.description: Specify the build number of the Jenkins job to retrieve its status and detailed information.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the build number of the Jenkins job to retrieve its status and detailed information.

operations.title: Execute an API Request
operations.description: Sends an API request to any API endpoint based on specified HTTP method, endpoint, and other input parameters that you have specified, enabling flexible API interactions tailored to user needs.

parameters.title: Endpoint URL
parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.description: Specify the target API URL path for the request. For example, if the website is https://example.com and URL path is https://example.com/api/v2/incidents/search, the endpoint would be /api/v2/incidents/search.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the target API URL path for the request. For example, if the website is https://example.com and URL path is https://example.com/api/v2/incidents/search, the endpoint would be /api/v2/incidents/search.

parameters.name: method
parameters.title: Method
parameters.description: Select an HTTP action for the request. You can select from the following options: GET, DELETE, PATCH, POST, PUT.
parameters.tooltip: Select an HTTP action for the request. You can select from the following options: GET, DELETE, PATCH, POST, PUT.

parameters.title: Query Parameters
parameters.name: query_params
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify any optional parameters to add to the URL and refine the request.
parameters.tooltip: Specify any optional parameters to add to the URL and refine the request.

parameters.name: payload
parameters.title: Payload
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify data, as JSON, to be sent as the request payload (typically for POST or PUT requests).
parameters.tooltip: Specify data, as JSON, to be sent as the request payload (typically for POST or PUT requests).
